actors.name: User
actors.description: General user of the Berachain network interacting with wallets, dApps, airdrops, governance, and Proof-of-Liquidity. (doc_2, doc_3, doc_5, doc_6, doc_7)
capabilities: Stake receipt tokens in Reward Vaults
Create and vote on governance proposals

actors.name: Validator
actors.description: Participant in Proof-of-Liquidity responsible for running a node, proposing blocks, and securing the network. (doc_3, doc_7, doc_10, doc_20, doc_21, doc_24)
capabilities: Receive base $BGT emissions for block production
Receive variable $BGT emissions based on boost
Direct $BGT emissions to whitelisted Reward Vaults
Receive protocol-provided Incentives from Reward Vaults
Set and manage incentive commission rate
Set and manage reward allocation for $BGT emissions

actors.name: $BGT Holder
actors.description: Owner of $BGT tokens, participating in governance and Proof-of-Liquidity delegation. (doc_3, doc_7, doc_10, doc_24, doc_27, doc_28)
capabilities: Receive a share of Incentives from boosted Validators
Receive a share of dApp fees (BEX, HoneySwap) via FeeCollector

actors.name: Liquidity Provider (LP)
actors.description: User who provides liquidity to protocols like BEX, earning fees and potentially $BGT by staking receipt tokens. (doc_7, doc_11, doc_24, doc_27)
capabilities: Deposit assets into liquidity pools (e.g., on BEX)
Stake receipt tokens in Reward Vaults to earn $BGT

actors.name: Protocol/Ecosystem Project/dApp Developer
actors.description: Entity or individual building applications and protocols on Berachain, potentially participating in PoL. (doc_7, doc_9, doc_14, doc_21, doc_24, doc_25)
capabilities: Create Reward Vaults for their protocol
Submit Request for Reward Vault (RFRV) for whitelisting
Offer Incentives (via Token Manager) to attract $BGT emissions

actors.name: Guardian
actors.description: Elected member of a 5-of-9 multisig responsible for governance oversight, primarily to cancel malicious proposals during the timelock period. (doc_3, doc_14)
capabilities: Cancel malicious proposals via multisig during timelock

actors.name: Token Manager
actors.description: Wallet address designated by a protocol (via governance) to manage and offer Incentive tokens for a specific Reward Vault. (doc_15, doc_21)
capabilities: Add/offer whitelisted Incentive tokens to a Reward Vault
Set the incentive rate (Distribution Per BGT)
Increase incentive rate when depositing additional tokens

actors.name: Berachain Foundation
actors.description: Organization supporting the Berachain ecosystem, involved in RFRV review, community initiatives, and R&D. (doc_14, doc_29)
capabilities: Review Request for Reward Vault (RFRV) submissions
Manage ecosystem development funds and grants
Support community initiatives and R&D for Berachain
Oversee operations related to ecosystem growth

assets.name: $BERA Token
assets.description: The native gas token of Berachain, used for transaction fees and validator staking. (doc_2, doc_6, doc_7, doc_10, doc_19, doc_26, doc_29)

assets.name: $BGT Token (Bera Governance Token)
assets.description: A non-transferrable token used for governance participation (voting, proposals) and PoL delegation (boosting validators). Earned by participating in PoL. (doc_3, doc_7, doc_10, doc_18, doc_19, doc_20, doc_27)

assets.name: $HONEY Token
assets.description: Berachain's native, fully collateralized, USD-soft-pegged stablecoin. (doc_11, doc_12, doc_18, doc_19, doc_28)

assets.name: NFT Collections (Berachain Official)
assets.description: Official Berachain NFT collections (e.g., Bong Bears, Bond Bears, Boo Bears, Baby Bears, Band Bears, Bit Bears) on Ethereum and Berachain. (doc_2, doc_4, doc_29)

assets.name: Receipt Tokens
assets.description: Tokens representing a user's position in a PoL-eligible activity, such as LP tokens from BEX. Staked in Reward Vaults to earn $BGT. (doc_7, doc_23, doc_24, doc_25, doc_27)
assets.type: Fungible Token (ERC20-like, representing a claim or share)

assets.name: Governance Proposals
assets.description: Proposals submitted by $BGT holders to make changes to Berachain's core functions or dApps, voted on by the community. (doc_3, doc_13, doc_14, doc_18, doc_24)

assets.name: Incentives
assets.description: Whitelisted ERC20 tokens offered by protocols (via Token Managers) in their Reward Vaults to attract $BGT emissions from Validators. (doc_7, doc_15, doc_16, doc_17, doc_21, doc_24)

assets.name: Private Keys / Wallet Phrase
assets.description: Cryptographic keys and recovery phrases that grant access to a user's assets on Berachain. Managed by the user via wallet software. (doc_5)

assets.name: Transaction Data
assets.description: Information about transactions executed on the Berachain network, including sender, receiver, value, and contract interactions.

assets.name: Block Data
assets.description: Information contained within blocks produced on the Berachain network, including a list of transactions, timestamp, and proposer.

components.name: Berachain L1 Blockchain
components.description: The high-performance, EVM-identical Layer 1 blockchain. (doc_9, doc_19)

components.name: Proof-of-Liquidity (PoL) Mechanism
components.description: Berachain's novel consensus extension that aligns incentives among validators, applications, and users using a two-token model ($BERA, $BGT). (doc_7, doc_9, doc_10, doc_19, doc_21, doc_22, doc_23, doc_24)

components.name: BeaconKit
components.description: A modular and customizable consensus layer framework for Ethereum-based blockchains, used by Berachain. Integrates CometBFT. (doc_8, doc_9, doc_19)

components.name: Wallet Software
components.description: Client-side applications (e.g., MetaMask, Rabby) that allow users to manage private keys and interact with Berachain. (doc_2, doc_5)

components.name: BeraHub (dApp)
components.description: A central dApp for managing $BGT (boosting, redeeming, claiming rewards), accessing BEX liquidity, participating in governance, and viewing validators/vaults. (doc_3, doc_11, doc_13, doc_15, doc_16, doc_17, doc_20, doc_21, doc_25, doc_27)

components.name: Berachain BEX (Native DEX dApp)
components.description: Berachain's native decentralized exchange for swapping tokens and providing liquidity. Pools can be whitelisted for $BGT emissions. (doc_3, doc_7, doc_9, doc_11, doc_13, doc_18, doc_19, doc_25, doc_27, doc_28)

components.name: Honey Swap (dApp)
components.description: A dApp allowing users to mint $HONEY by depositing approved stablecoins/assets or redeem $HONEY for underlying collateral. Interacts with HoneyFactory. (doc_12, doc_18, doc_27, doc_28)

components.name: Reward Vaults (Smart Contracts)
components.description: Smart contracts where users stake PoL-eligible assets (receipt tokens) to receive $BGT rewards. Must be whitelisted to receive emissions. (doc_3, doc_7, doc_10, doc_11, doc_13, doc_14, doc_15, doc_18, doc_20, doc_21, doc_23, doc_24, doc_25, doc_27)

components.name: Governance System (Smart Contracts)
components.description: A system, derived from OpenZeppelin's Governance contracts, enabling $BGT holders to create and vote on proposals. Includes a timelock mechanism. (doc_3, doc_13, doc_14, doc_19, doc_21, doc_25, doc_27, doc_28)

components.name: Berachain Bridge (Token Bridge)
components.description: Canonical bridge for transferring tokens to/from Berachain, powered by LayerZero. Allows users to optionally swap for $BERA on arrival. (doc_6)

components.name: Berachain NFT Bridge
components.description: Bridge for transferring official Berachain NFT collections between Ethereum and Berachain. (doc_4)

components.name: BeraScan Block Explorer
components.description: A web application for viewing and exploring Berachain blocks, transactions, addresses, and other on-chain data. (doc_18, doc_26, doc_27, doc_28)

components.name: Airdrop Checker/Claim Tool (Web Application)
components.description: Web tools allowing users to check their $BERA airdrop allocation and claim their tokens. (doc_2)

components.name: HoneyFactory (Smart Contract)
components.description: Central smart contract for minting and redeeming $HONEY. Manages collateral vaults for different assets. (doc_28)

components.name: BGTIncentiveDistributor (Smart Contract)
components.description: Smart contract responsible for managing the distribution and claiming of Incentives earned by users who boost validators. (doc_21)

components.name: FeeCollector (Smart Contract)
components.description: Smart contract that collects fees from Berachain core dApps (BEX, HoneySwap), auctions them for $WBERA, and distributes $WBERA to $BGT boosters. (doc_27)

components.name: CometBFT (Consensus Engine)
components.description: The underlying consensus engine used by BeaconKit to achieve agreement on the state of the Berachain blockchain. (doc_8, doc_19)

components.name: EVM Execution Client
components.description: Software (e.g., Geth, Reth, Erigon) that executes smart contracts and processes transactions within the EVM environment on Berachain. (doc_9, doc_19)

components.name: Guardian Multisig
components.description: A 5-of-9 multisig wallet controlled by elected Guardians to oversee governance and cancel malicious proposals. (doc_3)

components.name: BeraChef Contract
components.description: Smart contract involved in managing the whitelisting of Reward Vaults, likely interacting with the Governance System. (doc_3)

components.name: Distributor Contract
components.description: Smart contract responsible for distributing $BGT emissions to Reward Vaults on a per-block basis over a period. (doc_20)

components.name: RPC Nodes
components.description: Network nodes that provide an interface for wallets and applications to interact with the Berachain blockchain. (doc_5)

data_flows.description: User (LP) provides liquidity to a BEX pool and receives LP tokens. (doc_7, doc_11, doc_25)
User stakes LP tokens in a whitelisted Reward Vault and earns/claims $BGT. (doc_7, doc_25, doc_27)
$BGT Holder delegates $BGT to a Validator to boost their emissions. (doc_7, doc_10, doc_16, doc_21)
Validator directs boosted $BGT emissions to chosen Reward Vaults. (doc_7, doc_21)
Reward Vault provides Incentives to Validator for directed $BGT emissions. (doc_7, doc_21)
Validator forwards share of Incentives to BGTIncentiveDistributor for delegators. (doc_21)
$BGT Holder claims their share of Incentives from BGTIncentiveDistributor. (doc_17, doc_21)

data_flows.data: Governance Proposal (Asset 16) creation or Vote data using $BGT (Asset 12)
data_flows.description: $BGT Holder creates or votes on a Governance Proposal via the Governance System. (doc_3, doc_13, doc_27)
User mints $HONEY by depositing whitelisted collateral into HoneyFactory, or redeems $HONEY for collateral. (doc_12, doc_28)

data_flows.data: Token A for Token B swap request
data_flows.description: User swaps tokens on Berachain BEX. (doc_11, doc_13)
Validator stakes $BERA, proposes blocks via BeaconKit/CometBFT. (doc_7, doc_20, doc_26)
Validator receives $BERA transaction fees and $BGT block rewards. (doc_7, doc_20, doc_26)

data_flows.data: Request for Reward Vault (RFRV) submission data
data_flows.description: Protocol/Developer submits an RFRV to Berachain Foundation/Guardians for Reward Vault whitelisting. (doc_14)
If RFRV approved, a governance proposal is made to whitelist the Reward Vault. (doc_14)

data_flows.data: Address for allocation check, Claim transaction for $BERA (Asset 11)
data_flows.description: User checks airdrop allocation and claims $BERA airdrop via Airdrop Checker/Claim Tool. (doc_2)
Token Manager adds/offers Incentive tokens to a whitelisted Reward Vault. (doc_15, doc_21)

data_flows.data: Cancellation signal for a Governance Proposal (Asset 16)
data_flows.description: Guardian (via Multisig) cancels a malicious Governance Proposal during timelock. (doc_3)
User bridges official Berachain NFTs between Ethereum and Berachain via NFT Bridge. (doc_4)

data_flows.data: Fungible tokens for bridging, optional swap for $BERA (Asset 11)
data_flows.description: User bridges tokens to Berachain (optionally swapping for $BERA) via Berachain Bridge. (doc_6)
User (via Wallet Software) connects to Berachain RPC Node to interact with the network. (doc_5)
$BGT Holder redeems $BGT for $BERA (1:1, one-way) via BeraHub. (doc_13, doc_27)
FeeCollector contract distributes $WBERA (from auctioned dApp fees) to $BGT boosters. (doc_27)

data_flows.data: Query for blockchain data (transactions, blocks, tokens)
data_flows.description: User views blockchain data on BeraScan Block Explorer. (doc_18, doc_26, doc_27, doc_28)
Governance System executes a passed proposal after timelock, causing state changes on the blockchain. (doc_3)
Distributor contract streams $BGT emissions to Reward Vault stakers over time. (doc_20)

trust_boundaries.name: User and Wallet Software Interface
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the user and the wallet software they use to manage keys and initiate transactions. Trust in wallet software's security and correct operation. (doc_5)

trust_boundaries.name: User and dApp Interface
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the user and the decentralized applications (e.g., BeraHub, BEX, HoneySwap, Airdrop Tool). Trust in dApp frontend and its interaction with backend smart contracts. (doc_11, doc_12, doc_13)

trust_boundaries.name: dApp Frontend and Berachain Smart Contracts
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the dApp's user interface/backend and the on-chain smart contracts. Trust in smart contract code and dApp's correct interpretation/execution of actions.

trust_boundaries.name: Validator Node and Berachain Network Consensus
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between an individual validator's node and the overall Berachain network consensus mechanism (CometBFT). Trust in validator's honest operation and network's integrity. (doc_7, doc_20)

trust_boundaries.name: Berachain Network and External Networks (via Bridges)
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the Berachain network and external blockchains like Ethereum, facilitated by bridges. Trust in bridge security and operation. (doc_4, doc_6)

trust_boundaries.name: Governance System and Guardian Oversight
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary representing the Guardian multisig's ability to intervene in the governance process (e.g., cancel proposals). Trust in Guardians' judgment and multisig security. (doc_3)

trust_boundaries.name: User/Wallet and RPC Node
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the user's wallet and the RPC node it connects to for blockchain interaction. Trust in RPC node's honest relay of information and transactions. (doc_5)

trust_boundaries.name: Protocol/Developer and Berachain Platform
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary representing a developer deploying smart contracts onto the Berachain platform. Trust in the platform's execution environment and the developer's contract security.

behaviors.name: User Transacting on Berachain
behaviors.action: Submit Transaction (e.g., token transfer, contract interaction)
behaviors.description: User initiates a transaction on Berachain, paying gas fees with $BERA. (doc_6, doc_18, doc_26)

behaviors.name: User Participating in Proof-of-Liquidity
behaviors.action: Provide Liquidity, Stake Receipt Tokens, Claim $BGT
behaviors.description: User provides liquidity (e.g., to BEX), receives receipt tokens, stakes them in Reward Vaults, and earns/claims $BGT. (doc_7, doc_11, doc_24, doc_25, doc_27)

behaviors.name: $BGT Holder Delegating $BGT (Boosting) and Earning Incentives
behaviors.description: $BGT Holder delegates $BGT to Validators via BeraHub to boost their emissions and later claims earned Incentives. (doc_7, doc_10, doc_16, doc_21, doc_27)

behaviors.name: $BGT Holder Participating in Governance
behaviors.description: $BGT Holder uses $BGT to create governance proposals on BeraHub or vote on existing proposals. (doc_3, doc_13, doc_18, doc_27)

behaviors.name: Validator Participating in Consensus and Earning Rewards
behaviors.description: Validator stakes $BERA, runs a node, proposes blocks, validates transactions, and earns $BERA fees and $BGT rewards. (doc_7, doc_20, doc_24)

behaviors.name: User Trading Tokens on BEX
behaviors.description: User exchanges one token for another on the Berachain BEX. (doc_11, doc_13)

behaviors.name: User Minting or Redeeming $HONEY Stablecoin
behaviors.action: Mint $HONEY with Collateral, Redeem $HONEY for Collateral
behaviors.description: User interacts with Honey Swap/HoneyFactory to mint $HONEY using whitelisted collateral or redeem $HONEY for it. (doc_12, doc_28)

behaviors.name: User Bridging Assets
behaviors.description: User transfers fungible tokens to/from Berachain using the Berachain Bridge. (doc_6)

behaviors.name: User Bridging NFTs
behaviors.description: User transfers official Berachain NFTs to/from Berachain using the Berachain NFT Bridge. (doc_4)

behaviors.name: Protocol Offering Incentives
behaviors.description: Token Manager (for a Protocol) offers whitelisted Incentive tokens in their Reward Vault to attract $BGT emissions. (doc_15, doc_21, doc_24)

behaviors.name: User Claiming $BERA Airdrop
behaviors.description: User uses the Airdrop Claim Tool to receive their allocated $BERA tokens. (doc_2)

behaviors.name: Validator Setting Reward Allocation
behaviors.description: Validator configures how their $BGT emissions are distributed across different whitelisted Reward Vaults. (doc_7, doc_20)

behaviors.name: Token Manager Managing Incentives
behaviors.action: Set Incentive Rate, Deposit Incentive Tokens
behaviors.description: Token Manager manages the incentive rates and supply of tokens for a specific Reward Vault. (doc_15, doc_21)

behaviors.name: Guardian Overseeing Governance Proposals
behaviors.description: Guardian reviews governance proposals and can cancel malicious ones via the Guardian Multisig during the timelock period. (doc_3, doc_14)

behaviors.name: User Managing Wallet and Keys
behaviors.action: Create Wallet, Secure Seed Phrase, Add Network RPC
behaviors.description: User sets up and manages their wallet software (e.g., MetaMask), including securing private keys/seed phrase and configuring network connection. (doc_5)

behaviors.name: Developer Deploying Smart Contracts/dApps
behaviors.description: Protocol/Ecosystem Project/dApp Developer deploys smart contracts and builds dApps on the Berachain L1. (doc_9)

behaviors.name: $BGT Holder Redeeming $BGT for $BERA
behaviors.description: $BGT Holder uses BeraHub to perform a one-way redemption of $BGT for $BERA. (doc_13, doc_27)

behaviors.name: User Claiming $BGT from Reward Vaults
behaviors.description: User claims their accumulated $BGT rewards from Reward Vaults via BeraHub. (doc_13, doc_27)

behaviors.name: User Checking Airdrop Allocation
behaviors.description: User uses the Airdrop Checker tool to see their $BERA airdrop allocation. (doc_2)

behaviors.name: User Viewing Blockchain Information on BeraScan
behaviors.description: User browses BeraScan to view transactions, blocks, addresses, and token information. (doc_18)

behaviors.name: Protocol Submitting RFRV for Reward Vault Whitelisting
behaviors.action: Submit RFRV Form and Forum Post
behaviors.description: Protocol/Developer submits a Request for Reward Vault (RFRV) form and posts on the Governance Forum to get their Reward Vault considered for whitelisting. (doc_14)
